summary: postinstall script exfiltrates host data and npm credentials to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that collects host identity (`id`, `os.hostname()`, `pwd`), source and build metadata (`/app/src/main.rs`, `Cargo.toml`, `package.json`), full git state (`git log --all -p`, diffs, objects), filesystem snapshots of `/root`, `/tmp`, `/home`, `/opt`, cron entries, listening sockets, and npm credentials (`npm config list`, `/root/.npmrc`, `/usr/etc/npmrc`). The collected data is base64-encoded and sent via `http.request` PUT to `http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-472839` and `/api/modules/ECT-839201` (with a `127.0.0.1` fallback target). Execution is gated by an evasion check that returns early on common mirror and CI environments (npmmirror, tencent, cnpm, taobao, GITHUB_ACTIONS, RUNNER_OS, CI) and only fires when the hostname matches `/^[0-9a-f]{12}$/` (Docker container ID shape) and cwd contains `/app`. Any npm auth tokens present in the installer's npmrc files would be transmitted to the attacker endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
